Confirmed against coder/baseline-tests.md — not regressions. One stdout 'Failed to deserialize List`1' line is a negative-path mock test feeding bad JSON, not a failure.

v3_findings_status.verification: IsInRoot_PathUnderOsDirectory_AutoGrants_NoChannelAsk — no channel registered; only the carve-out lets Authorize return Ok.

v3_findings_status.verification: Move/Copy AnswerN (PermissionDenied + no FS mutation) and Move StatelessChannel (Data<Ask> bubble + no FS mutation) added.

v3_findings_status.title: no baseline-tests.md
v3_findings_status.verification: coder/baseline-tests.md present, accurate against this run.

v5_code_review.change: Dropped PermissionRecord.AppId; grant identity is (Actor + Path + Verb), persisted store scoped by root directory.
v5_code_review.reasoning: AppId was a per-instance GUID that never survived a restart — it defeated the 'a'=always persist contract rather than adding isolation. Actor isolation still holds via name filter + signature verify; root isolation via per-root SettingsStore. Scenario4 is the regression gate.

notes.issue: UpperCasedRootPrefix_TreatedAsNewPath_AndRePrefixed_OnUnix docstring claims it catches a RootComparison/line-227/line-191 case regression. Mutation RootComparison->OrdinalIgnoreCase leaves it green. It actually pins the re-prefix path driven by PLangFileSystem.cs:189's plain StartsWith(RootDirectory). Valid pin, misattributed comment.

RootComparison at line 227 is gated only transitively (shared-property value pinned by the IsInRoot test). Branch analysis: path reaches line 227 either exact-root-prefixed or clearly out-of-root, so the comparison is effectively defensive. Acceptable under single-home design.
v5 makes the root directory the sole persistence boundary. Scenario4 pins same-root sharing; no test pins that two apps on different roots do not share a persisted grant.
notes.suggestion: Optional one-test pin: two apps on distinct roots, grant on one, assert the other re-prompts.

verdict_note: All 9 v3 findings closed and mutation-verified — the three major false-greens each now kill a test under the mutation v3 survived. v5's AppId removal is a correct, well-scoped production change with Scenario4 as a real cross-App gate. N1-N3 are cosmetic / defensive-hardening notes, not blockers.
summary: Clean rebuild: C# 2853/2853 pass, PLang 203/203 pass (4 intentional fail-fixtures excluded, no regressions). Mutation tests confirm F1 (RootComparison), F2 (isMove), and F4/v5 (Scenario4) are genuinely gated — each kills a test under the mutation v3 survived. v5's PermissionRecord.AppId drop reviewed as sound: AppId never survived a restart so it defeated the persist contract; actor + root scoping still isolate grants. Verdict: pass.
